children.name: snmpOutGetResponses
children.description:
The total number of SNMP Get-Response PDUs which
            have been generated by the SNMP protocol entity.

children.name: snmpOutBadValues
children.description:
The total number of SNMP PDUs which were generated
            by the SNMP protocol entity and for which the value
            of the error-status field was `badValue'.

children.name: snmpInSetRequests
children.description:
The total number of SNMP Set-Request PDUs which
            have been accepted and processed by the SNMP protocol
            entity.

children.name: snmpOutTooBigs
children.description:
The total number of SNMP PDUs which were generated
            by the SNMP protocol entity and for which the value
            of the error-status field was `tooBig.'

children.name: snmpInTooBigs
children.description:
The total number of SNMP PDUs which were
            delivered to the SNMP protocol entity and for
            which the value of the error-status field was
            `tooBig'.

children.name: snmpInTraps
children.description:
The total number of SNMP Trap PDUs which have been
            accepted and processed by the SNMP protocol entity.

children.name: snmpOutGetRequests
children.description:
The total number of SNMP Get-Request PDUs which
            have been generated by the SNMP protocol entity.

children.name: snmpOutGetNexts
children.description:
The total number of SNMP Get-Next PDUs which have
            been generated by the SNMP protocol entity.

children.name: snmpInReadOnlys
children.description:
The total number valid SNMP PDUs which were delivered
            to the SNMP protocol entity and for which the value
            of the error-status field was `readOnly'.  It should
            be noted that it is a protocol error to generate an
            SNMP PDU which contains the value `readOnly' in the
            error-status field, as such this object is provided
            as a means of detecting incorrect implementations of
            the SNMP.

children.name: snmpInNoSuchNames
children.description:
The total number of SNMP PDUs which were
            delivered to the SNMP protocol entity and for
            which the value of the error-status field was
            `noSuchName'.

children.name: sysObjectID
children.description:
The vendor's authoritative identification of the
            network management subsystem contained in the entity.
            This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises
            subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and
            unambiguous means for determining `what kind of box' is
            being managed.  For example, if vendor `Flintstones,
            Inc.' was assigned the subtree 1.3.6.1.4.1.424242,
            it could assign the identifier 1.3.6.1.4.1.424242.1.1
            to its `Fred Router'.

children.name: sysORLastChange
children.description:
The value of sysUpTime at the time of the most recent
            change in state or value of any instance of sysORID.

children.name: sysORTable
children.description:
The (conceptual) table listing the capabilities of
            the local SNMP application acting as a command
            responder with respect to various MIB modules.
            SNMP entities having dynamically-configurable support
            of MIB modules will have a dynamically-varying number
            of conceptual rows.

children.name: sysUpTime
children.description:
The time (in hundredths of a second) since the
            network management portion of the system was last
            re-initialized.

children.name: snmpSetSerialNo
children.description:
An advisory lock used to allow several cooperating
            command generator applications to coordinate their
            use of the SNMP set operation.

            This object is used for coarse-grain coordination.
            To achieve fine-grain coordination, one or more similar
            objects might be defined within each MIB group, as
            appropriate.
